title: lepidomicrosorium hederaceum
intro: Lepidomicrosorium hederaceum, also known as the creeping maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to tropical regions and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used in herbal preparations to treat a range of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, and has been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Lepidomicrosorium hederaceum is often used in traditional medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures.
